segregr 0.1.0
  • Get started
  • Reference
  • Changelog

    Collect all local segregation metrics into a spatial sf object

    local_metrics_to_sf.Rd

    Combines local Dissimilarity, Entropy, H, and pairwise Exposure/Isolation into a single wide sf object, joining results back to the input areal unit geometries.

    local_metrics_to_sf(segregation_results, bandwidths = c())

    Arguments

    segregation_results

    a segreg object returned by measure_segregation.

    bandwidths

    numeric vector. Optional subset of bandwidths to include. When empty (default), all bandwidths are returned.

    Value

    a spatial sf object with one row per areal unit per bandwidth, containing columns id, bw, dissimilarity, entropy, h, and one column per pairwise exposure/isolation combination.

    Examples

    
library(sf)
library(segregr)

marilia_sf <- st_read(system.file("extdata/marilia_2010.gpkg",
                                  package = "segregr"))
#> Reading layer `marilia_2010_proj' from data source 
#>   `/home/runner/work/_temp/Library/segregr/extdata/marilia_2010.gpkg' 
#>   using driver `GPKG'
#> Simple feature collection with 295 features and 10 fields
#> Geometry type: POLYGON
#> Dimension:     XY
#> Bounding box:  xmin: 597725.2 ymin: 7535553 xmax: 612714.8 ymax: 7551388
#> Projected CRS: SIRGAS 2000 / UTM zone 22S
segregation <- measure_segregation(marilia_sf)

local_metrics_to_sf(segregation)
#> Joining with `by = join_by(id, bw)`
#> Simple feature collection with 295 features and 86 fields
#> Geometry type: POLYGON
#> Dimension:     XY
#> Bounding box:  xmin: 597725.2 ymin: 7535553 xmax: 612714.8 ymax: 7551388
#> Projected CRS: SIRGAS 2000 / UTM zone 22S
#> First 10 features:
#>                 id bw dissimilarity  entropy             h
#> 1  352900505000001  0  0.0005470273 1.849289 -1.380548e-04
#> 2  352900505000002  0  0.0011001293 1.891925 -1.702489e-04
#> 3  352900505000003  0  0.0017888527 1.850513 -2.179233e-04
#> 4  352900505000004  0  0.0013975371 1.989858 -4.324360e-04
#> 5  352900505000005  0  0.0004308401 1.752560 -4.145553e-05
#> 6  352900505000006  0  0.0004429882 1.600521  3.709038e-04
#> 7  352900505000007  0  0.0002433143 1.743954 -1.725795e-05
#> 8  352900505000008  0  0.0003857752 1.572401  2.994662e-04
#> 9  352900505000009  0  0.0005230570 1.777405 -7.028603e-05
#> 10 352900505000010  0  0.0003233163 1.641489  9.289725e-05
#>    iso_mw_05_to_1_mw_05_to_1 exp_mw_05_to_1_mw_0_to_05
#> 1               3.049899e-04              4.574848e-05
#> 2               3.047101e-05              0.000000e+00
#> 3               3.124129e-05              0.000000e+00
#> 4               1.559238e-04              2.751597e-05
#> 5               8.377819e-04              3.723475e-05
#> 6               1.696003e-03              7.066680e-05
#> 7               5.184872e-04              3.345079e-05
#> 8               6.718253e-04              3.631488e-05
#> 9               2.496099e-04              2.377237e-05

#> 1                1.524949e-05               3.049899e-05
